name: Amsterdam
industries: Banking, travel and payments technology, and the Schiphol logistics complex drive one of Europe's most agile-mature markets.
intro: Amsterdam's claim on agile history is direct — ING's enterprise-wide agile reorganisation here became the model banks worldwide copied — and the city's delivery market still runs ahead of the continent. Tech firms along the Zuidas and in the harbour district, the payments and travel giants born here, and the airport-logistics complex at Schiphol all hire Scrum-credentialed professionals as standard; programme managers with PMP anchor the infrastructure and energy-transition portfolios. Dutch directness extends to hiring: the certificate is checked first. We run live online cohorts throughout the year on Amsterdam-friendly hours — weekend Saturday–Sunday batches and midweek Wednesday–Thursday batches both — meaning learners in Amsterdam attend at ordinary local times, never overnight; the table below shows each upcoming date converted for Amsterdam. Afterwards we support the exam application, drill full-length mocks, and keep the trainer on call for doubts until you pass.

faq.q: What are the class timings in Amsterdam?
faq.a: Live classes run 09:00 AM – 06:00 PM UK, which is 10:00 AM – 07:00 PM GMT+2 in Amsterdam. You can pick a weekend (Sat–Sun) or a weekday (Wed–Thu) cohort; each completes the course over two consecutive days.

faq.q: Is the training online or at a venue in Amsterdam?
faq.a: All batches are 100% live online, taught by an instructor over video with interactive exercises — there is no venue to travel to in Amsterdam. You need a laptop, a stable connection and a free weekend.

faq.q: Is the certificate different if I attend from Netherlands?
faq.a: No. The certification is issued by the same global body regardless of where you attend from, and holds identical recognition with employers in Netherlands and internationally.

faq.q: Can my employer in Amsterdam sponsor the course?
faq.a: Yes. We issue invoices suitable for corporate sponsorship and reimbursement, and most employers in Netherlands accept them under learning and development budgets. Group pricing is available for teams.
